ref: “Don't get in our way,” the crowd answered. “You're a foreigner. What right do you have to order us around? We'll do worse things to you than we're going to do to them.” The crowd kept arguing with Lot. Finally, they rushed toward the door to break it down.
text: Amaa ka Sɔdomnim’ maa yɛl’ o, “Miligim’ ka ti ti palo. A nyɛla sana kpe, ka bɔri ni a wuhi ti ti ni yɛn niŋ shɛm? A yi bi baŋ ti ni niŋ a din bie n-gari ti ni yɛn niŋ ba shɛm.” Ka bɛ daa kul dihiri Lɔt na hal ti paai dunoli m-bɔri ni bɛ ŋme n-kab’ li.
translated: Sodomnim said, "Miligi, quickly take your belongings. Since you have come here, what will you do with your things if the rain comes? You must take your children and go, for you will not find a place to stay there again." So, they quickly urged Lot to take his family and leave before the morning, so that he might not be caught in the disaster.
